title: guajava sorocabensis
intro: Guajava sorocabensis, also known as the Brazilian guava tree, is a species of tree in the Rubiaceae family that is native to Brazil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Guajava sorocabensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from pain and swelling. Guajava soracobensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and bark are often used in traditional folk medicine.
